notes: status reflects availability: DONE is implemented; OPEN/PARKED are roadmap intents.
install_modes are declared as intended delivery/activation modes; vendor_pack should only be added once an offline installer/bundler exists.

modules.title: Enforcement Pack v1 (contract + severity matrix + rules)
modules.summary: Contract-first enforcement-check schema + severity governance + Semgrep rule set. V1 is extension-only; wiring lives in V2.

evidence_hooks.description: Master demo pack + closeout exists for ENF v1 (contract+matrix+EP rules).

modules.title: ENF v2 wiring (ops command + pre-commit + CI)
modules.summary: Single-source-of-truth runner is wired into local advisory + CI staged enforcement.

evidence_hooks.description: Ops command, pre-commit, and CI wiring are applied and marked DONE in work_item_state (staged enforcement).

modules.title: ExecutorPort (vendor-neutral execution adapters)
modules.summary: ExecutorPort abstraction with local/manual default adapter; optional Codex adapter; offline-first.

evidence_hooks.description: Contract test exists and is expected to PASS under network OFF.

modules.title: Observability Pack (OTel optional)
modules.summary: trace_meta correlation (run_id) + optional OTel bridge; offline-first defaults.

evidence_hooks.description: Contract test exists and is expected to PASS under network OFF.

modules.title: MemoryPort baseline (local-first; optional adapters)
modules.summary: Local-first persistence is default; optional Qdrant/pgvector adapters are placeholders and never auto-connect under network OFF.

evidence_hooks.description: Contract test exists and is expected to PASS under network OFF.

modules.title: Offline-first installer/bundle (vendor_pack + provenance) — v1
modules.summary: Offline-first bundling strategy + installer semantics for vendor_pack delivery. This enables portable product distribution (no network).

evidence_hooks.description: Must produce a deterministic offline bundle + verification evidence (sha256/provenance) without enabling network.

modules.title: MemoryPort backend: Qdrant HTTP adapter (no-deps, network-gated)
modules.summary: Real Qdrant adapter via stdlib HTTP; must be network_mode-gated and fail-closed when OFF.

evidence_hooks.description: Must PASS contract test with network OFF (adapter unavailable + local_first ok) and network ON (adapter works when configured).

modules.title: MemoryPort backend: pgvector adapter (optional; deps-governed)
modules.summary: Optional Postgres/pgvector backend; requires dependency governance and deterministic SKIP/fail-closed when deps missing.

evidence_hooks.description: Requires dependency policy decision + adapter contract test PASS when deps/config present.

modules.title: Workflow adapter (Temporal) — PARKED/upsell
modules.summary: External workflow engine integration behind a vendor-neutral interface. Activate only when durable long-running workflows are needed.

evidence_hooks.description: Activation signals: durable long-running workflows, retries/compensation, scheduling, distributed workers.

modules.title: Data bridge adapter (Benthos) — PARKED/upsell
modules.summary: Streaming ingestion/connectors behind a vendor-neutral interface. Activate only when repeatable ingestion pipelines are needed.

evidence_hooks.description: Activation signals: connector demand, backpressure-aware pipelines, repeatable ETL feeding memory/index stores.

tiers.title: Community
tiers.description: Offline-first developer experience; core extensions and local baselines.

tiers.title: Pro
tiers.description: Adds staged enforcement wiring + productization foundation + optional online adapters.

tiers.title: Enterprise
tiers.description: Adds higher-risk integrations (DB backends, workflow engines, data bridges) when explicitly activated.

products.title: Core Offline Orchestrator
notes: No network required; focuses on deterministic evidence + contracts.

products.title: Enforcement Pack (Pro)
notes: Staged enforcement: default (onboarding) vs strict (blocking in CI).

demos.title: 5-minute ENF demo: enforcement-check default vs strict
